plan_review_notes: Approve the internal naming slice: canonical sisyphus submodule imports and internal symbol renames with compatibility aliases preserved, while keeping persisted/protocol identifiers out of scope.

plan_review_history.notes: Revised this task to the concrete internal naming slice: add canonical sisyphus submodule aliases, introduce SisyphusConfig as the canonical internal config type with TaskflowConfig as a compatibility alias, rename obvious internal helpers and test classes, and move tests/wrappers toward canonical sisyphus imports while preserving taskflow compatibility.
Approve the internal naming slice: canonical sisyphus submodule imports and internal symbol renames with compatibility aliases preserved, while keeping persisted/protocol identifiers out of scope.

spec_review_notes: Freeze scope to sisyphus submodule aliasing, SisyphusConfig canonical naming, helper/docstring/test renames, and wrapper/test import updates. Do not rename taskflow.event.v1, TF task ids, or remove taskflow compatibility imports in this task.

normal_cases.name: Canonical `sisyphus` submodule imports work for the main runtime modules and evolution package.
Internal config typing and helper names use canonical `Sisyphus` naming without breaking behavior.

edge_cases.name: Legacy `taskflow` imports continue to work after canonical aliasing is added.
Evolution imports work from the canonical `sisyphus.evolution` surface, not only from `taskflow.evolution`.

exception_cases.name: Persisted/protocol identifiers such as `taskflow.event.v1` and `TF-...` are not accidentally renamed.
Compatibility strings that must stay legacy are not broken while canonical names are introduced.

verification_methods.target: Canonical sisyphus submodule imports work for the main runtime modules and evolution package.
Internal config typing and helper names use canonical Sisyphus naming without breaking behavior.
Legacy taskflow imports continue to work and persisted identifiers remain unchanged.
Compatibility and persisted identifiers are not renamed accidentally.
